channel.title: Research on Kai — DeFi Research
channel.description: Recent content in Research on Kai — DeFi Research

item.title: LP-as-Equity
item.description:
Perp DEX competition started where most crypto competition starts: marketing. Hyperliquid’s success led many teams to optimize for large airdrop campaigns and token buybacks to align incentives with tokenholders. But as the category becomes more mature, Perps are commoditizing and competition is shifting from marketing into risk management. The challenge becomes which team can design the most capital-efficient risk engine.

Mechanically, Perp DEXs are credit facilities for leverage traders. LPs like HLP and JLP issue on-demand loans to leverage traders and receive a claim on interest income: fees, spread, and liquidation penalties. The vault’s design (market-making logic, spread, oracles) determines how each position is priced and marked, effectively setting the terms for loans. NAV reprices on whether the system mitigates adverse selection while capturing spread —a proxy for the team’s operational efficiency.

item.title: Aave: The Bank of DeFi
item.description: @aave has grown to become DeFi’s Trojan Horse, reaching a scale comparable to traditional banks. With $35 billion in TVL and $57 billion in net deposits, it now controls 25% of DeFi TVL and ranks among the 50 largest US banks by assets under management (AUM).
